content: The number of terminal and bridging hydrogens in $\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$ are respectively
explanation:
In diborane structure $${B_2}{H_6}$$ there are four $$2c-2e$$ bonds and two $$3c-2e$$ bonds (see structure of diborane).



Note : 2c - 2e bond means two center two electron bond and 3c - 2e bond means three center two electron bond.
Here number of $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{P}$ linkages $=6$

content: The number(s) of $$-\mathrm{OH}$$ group(s) present in $$\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3$$ and $$\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$$ is/are
The correct order of acidity of the following hydra acids is
explanation:
The stability of these halides decreases down in the group due to decrease in bond dissociation enthalpy.

Thus, the correct order of acidity is

$$\mathrm{HF < HCl < HBr < HI}$$.

content: Cyclo [18] carbon is an allotrope of carbon with molecular formula C<sub>18</sub>. It is a ring of 18 carbon atoms, connected by single and triple bonds. The total number of triple bonds present in this cyclocarbon are

Cl2O7 is the anhydride of HClO4. It reacts with water slowly to give perchloric acid.

$$C{l_2}{O_7} + {H_2}O \to \mathop {2HCl{O_4}}\limits_{\text{Perchloric acid}} $$

Its structure is as follows :


It is less reactive than other oxides of chlorine and does not react with P, S, coal or paper at room temperature.

content: Which of the following is present in maximum amount in 'acid rain'?
explanation:
H2SO4 (sulphuric acid) is present in maximum amount in 'acid rain'. Oxides of nitrogen and sulphur, released into the atmosphere from thermal power plants, industries and automobiles are the main sources of acid rain. These oxides on oxidation followed by hydrolysis give sulphuric acid and nitric acid that alongwith HCl are responsible for the acidity of rain. The oxidation reaction is catalysed by particulate matter present in the polluted atmosphere.

$$2S{O_2}(g) + {O_2}(g) + 2{H_2}O(l) \to 2{H_2}S{O_4}(aq)$$

$$4N{O_2}(g) + {O_2}(g) + 2{H_2}O(l) \to 4HN{O_3}(aq)$$

content: Which of the set of oxides are arranged in the proper order of basic, amphoteric, acidic?
explanation:
The correct set of oxides arranged in the proper order of basic, amphoteric, acidic are BaO, Al2O3, SO2.

Oxides of non-metallic elements are acidic such as CO2, NO2, SO2, etc. Oxides of less electropositive elements (such as BeO, Al2O3, Bi2O3, ZnO etc.) are amphoteric i.e. these behaves as acids toward strong bases on as bases towards strong acids. Oxides of electropositive elements (Na2O, CaO, Tl2O, BaO etc.) are basic and contain discrete O2$$-$$ ions.

content: When BaCl<sub>2</sub> is added to an aqueous salt solution, a white precipitate is obtained. The anion among CO$$_3^{2 - }$$, SO$$_3^{2 - }$$ and SO$$_4^{2 - }$$ that was present in the solution can be

options.content: CO$$_3^{2 - }$$ but not any of the other two
SO$$_3^{2 - }$$ but not any of the other two
SO$$_4^{2 - }$$ but not any of the other two

explanation: $$\because$$ CO$$_3^{2 - }$$, SO$$_3^{2 - }$$ and SO$$_4^{2 - }$$ are present along with BaCl<sub>2</sub>, these can also show white precipitate (as precipitate of all these are also white).

content: Nitrogen dioxide is not produced on heating
explanation:
Only KNO3 on heating do not given NO2 but all other give

(a) 2KNO3 $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ 2KNO2 + O2

(b) 2Pb(NO3)2 $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ 2PbO + 4NO2 + O2

(c) 2Cu(NO3)2 $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ 2CuO + 4NO2 + O2

(d) 2AgNO3 $$\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_{{\mathop{\rm Red}\nolimits} \,hot}^\Delta } $$ 2Ag + O2 + 2NO2

Heavy metal nitrates liberate nitrogen dioxide on heating.

content: Reaction of silver nitrate solution with phosphorus acid produces
explanation:
Reaction of silver nitrate solution with phosphorus acid produces phosphoric acid and metallic silver. Complete reaction is as follows:

$$\mathop {{H_3}P{O_3}}\limits_{Phosphorus\,acid} + AgN{O_3} + {H_2}O \to \mathop {{H_3}P{O_4}}\limits_{Phosphoric\,acid} + Ag \downarrow + \,2HN{O_3}$$

content: Which of the following statement(s) is/are incorrect?

options.content: A sink of SO<sub>2</sub> pollutant is O<sub>3</sub> in the atmosphere
FGD is a process of removing NO<sub>2</sub> from atmosphere
NO<sub>x</sub> in fuel gases can be removed by alkaline scrubbing
The catalyst used to convert CCl<sub>4</sub> to CF<sub>4</sub> by HF is SbF<sub>5</sub>

explanation:
Statements (a, b and d) are incorrect whereas statement (c) is correct. The corrected form are as follows :

$$ \bullet $$ A sink of SO2 pollutant is ocean.

$$ \bullet $$ FGD (Flue-gas desulfurisation) is a process of removing SO2 from atmosphere.

$$ \bullet $$ The catalyst used to convert CCl4 to CF4 by HF is AgF.

So, the correct options are (a), (b) and (d).

content: SiO<sub>2</sub> is attacked by which one/ones of the following?
explanation:
SiO2 is attacked by HF, hot NaOH and fluorine. Reactions involved are as follows :

(a) $$Si{O_2}(s) + 6HF(aq) \to H2Si{F_6}(aq) + 2{H_2}O(l)$$

(c) $$Si{O_2}(s) + (hot)\,2NaOH(aq) \to N{a_2}Si{O_3}(s) + {H_2}O(l)$$

(d) $$Si{O_2}(s) + {F_2}(g) \to Si{F_4}(aq) + {O_2}(g)$$

So, the correct options are (a), (c) and (d).

content: Which of the following chemicals may be used to identify three unlabelled beakers containing conc. NaOH, conc. H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> and water?
explanation:
(a) NH4NO3 will evolve NH3 pungent smelling gas with NaOH.

$$\bullet$$ NO2 brown gas with con. H2SO4

$$\bullet$$ No reaction with water

Thus, NH4NO3 can be used to label all three beakers.

(c) (NH4)2CO3 will evolve pungent smelling gas NH3 with NaOH.

$$\bullet$$ Effervescence of CO2 gas with con. H2SO4

$$\bullet$$ No reaction with water.

Thus, (NH4)2CO3 can be used to label all three beakers.

Thus, options (a, c) are correct.

content: White phosphorus P<sub>4</sub> has the following characteristics
explanation: White phosphorus (P4) has 6P $$-$$ P single bonds, 4 lone pairs of electrons and P $$-$$ P $$-$$ P angle of 60$$^\circ$$. It is a translucent white waxy solid. It has large atomic size and less electronegativity. So, it forms single bond instead of p$$\pi$$ - p$$\pi$$ multiple bond. It consists of discrete tetrahedral P4 molecule as shown in the figure.

content: Which of the following statement(s) is (are) correct when a mixture of NaCl and K<sub>2</sub>Cr<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub> is gently warmed with conc. H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>?

options.content: A deep red vapour is evolved
The vapour when passed through NaOH solution, gives a yellow solution

explanation:
(A) When NaCl and K2Cr2O7 warmed with H2SO4 (i.e. in acidic medium), they produce deep red vapours of chromyl chloride (CrO2Cl2).

(B) When NaOH is passed, the product CrO3 formed in step (a) will react with NaOH and gives yellow colour solution.

(C) Chlorine gas is not evolved, thus false.

(D) In the given reaction, chromyl chloride (CrO2Cl2) is formed. All reactions are as follows:

(i) K2Cr2O7 + 2H2SO4 $$\to$$ 2KHSO4 + 2CrO3 + H2O

(ii) NaCl + H2SO4 $$\to$$ NaHSO4 + HCl

(iii) $$\mathop {Cr{O_3}}\limits_{from\,(i)} + \mathop {2HCl}\limits_{from\,(ii)} \to \mathop {Cr{O_2}C{l_2}}\limits_{{\mathop{\rm Re}\nolimits} d\,vapours} $$

(iv) CrO2Cl2 + 2NaOH $$\to$$ $$\mathop {N{a_2}Cr{O_4}}\limits_{Yellow\,solution} $$ + 2HCl
